region.description: AWS region to use for authentication (e.g., us-west-2, us-east-1)
region.title: Region

additional-make-args.description: A list of additional arguments to pass to adbc-make.
additional-make-args.title: Additional-Make-Args

go-mod-path.description: Path containing the go.mod file for Go drivers. Used to cache dependencies in CI. Defaults to the language subdir.
go-mod-path.title: Go-Mod-Path

lang-tools.description: Install tools for these languages to use in the build.
lang-tools.title: Lang-Tools

environment-contexts.description: What parts of the build require the environment.
environment-contexts.title: Environment-Contexts

subdir.description: Override the default subdirectory for this language. Use '.' to place files at the repository root.
subdir.title: Subdir

skip-test.description: Whether to skip test workflows (primarily useful for build-only drivers)
skip-test.title: Skip-Test

skip-validate.description: Whether to skip the validation suite in CI (this should only be used temporarily while setting up a driver)
skip-validate.title: Skip-Validate

SecretConfigDict.description: Secret configuration with explicit secret name and contexts.

secret.description: The name of the GitHub secret to use for this secret variable
secret.title: Secret

contexts.description: Workflow contexts where this secret should be available
contexts.title: Contexts

SecretConfigDict.title: SecretConfigDict

extra-dependencies.description:
Additional dependencies to install for validation workflows. Specify as key-value pairs. Example:

[validation.extra-dependencies]
pytest = ">=7,<8"
black = "*"
extra-dependencies.title: Extra-Dependencies

extra-pypi-dependencies.description:
Additional dependencies to install for validation workflows. Specify as key-value pairs. Example:

[validation.extra-pypi-dependencies]
pytest = ">=7,<8"
black = "*"
extra-pypi-dependencies.title: Extra-Pypi-Dependencies

description: You can validate your generate.toml against this schema with tools like tombi (https://tombi-toml.github.io/tombi/docs/linter). Requires placing a `#:schema` directive at the top of your generate.toml file.

repository.description: GitHub repository name (if different from driver name)
repository.title: Repository

driver.description: Driver name. Should be lowercase (e.g., postgresql, sqlite)
driver.title: Driver

environment.description: Name of a GitHub Actions Environment to use when including secrets in workflows
environment.title: Environment

private.description: Whether the driver is private. Most drivers will be not be private so you can omit this.
private.title: Private

lang.description:
Programming language(s) to enable workflows for. Only go and rust are supported. Keys should be lowercase. Set to true to enable with default config, or false (the default) to disable. Example:

[lang]
go = true

[lang.rust.build]
additional-make-args = ["example"]
lang.title: Lang

secrets.description:
Secrets to enable in workflows. By default, no secrets are available in your generated workflows unless you specify them here.

To make a secret available in all workflows, use the simple syntax:

[secrets]
MY_TOKEN = "GITHUB_SECRET_NAME"

If you want more fine-grained control, you can restrict secrets to specific workflows. To do this, specify contexts contexts ('build:release', 'test', 'validate') like this:

[secrets.DB_PASSWORD]
secret = "TEST_DB_SECRET"
contexts = ["test", "validate"]
secrets.title: Secrets

aws.description:
Enables AWS authentication in workflows. Automatically adds AWS_ROLE and AWS_ROLE_SESSION_NAME secrets, and sets id_token permissions. Example:

[aws]
region = "us-west-2"

gcloud.description:
Enables Google Cloud authentication in workflows. Automatically adds GCLOUD_SERVICE_ACCOUNT and GCLOUD_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER secrets, and sets id_token permissions. Set to true to enable. Example:

gcloud = true
gcloud.title: Gcloud

validation.description: Configuration for validation workflows. Currently supports specifying extra dependencies.

title: Schema for generate.toml
